resource.note: tight — reserve for higher-acuity now-tier patients
single MD covering — prioritize now-tier first

warnings: Sepsis screen positive: T 39.4 (>38) + HR 98 (>90) — 2 SIRS criteria with suspected pneumonia
Three now-tier patients ahead in queue; ensure this patient is not lost in soon-tier backlog

hypotheses.name: Community-acquired pneumonia (right lower lobe)
supporting_signals: right lower lobe crackles on exam

hypotheses.name: Sepsis (pneumonia source)
supporting_signals: suspected pulmonary infection — meets 2 SIRS criteria

monitor.action: CBC, BMP, lactate, blood cultures x2, CXR; reassess vitals q30min including SpO2 trend
monitor.reason: Workup for pneumonia + sepsis screen; lactate clarifies severity tier.

monitor.action: IV access, antipyretic (acetaminophen), start empiric CAP antibiotics within 1h if sepsis criteria persist
monitor.reason: Two SIRS criteria with suspected pneumonia — meets sepsis screen threshold per R-SEPSIS-01.

escalate_or_redirect.action: Flag to charge nurse: sepsis-screen-positive in soon-tier — protect against queue drift
escalate_or_redirect.reason: Conservative bias: borderline ESI 2 vs 3; ensure 1h antibiotic clock not missed behind 3 now-tier cases.

explanation.summary: 29M with 4 days of fever (39.4), productive cough, and focal RLL crackles — clinical pneumonia. Vitals stable (BP 118/72, SpO2 96%, RR 18) but meets 2 SIRS criteria (T>38, HR>90 borderline at 98) with a suspected infectious source, so the sepsis screen is positive and needs labs + antibiotics within the 1h window. No AMS, hypotension, or hypoxia → does not meet ESI 2 high-risk threshold. Tier: soon (ESI 3) — needs ≥2 resources (labs + imaging + IV abx) but is hemodynamically stable. Asthma history warrants respiratory monitoring but no current bronchospasm.

citations.signal: stable vitals, needs labs + CXR + IV abx (≥2 resources)
suspected pneumonia + T>38 + HR>90 → sepsis screen positive, abx within 1h

checklist.task: Place IV, draw CBC/BMP/lactate/blood cultures x2
Start empiric CAP antibiotics if sepsis criteria persist after lactate result
Reassess vitals q30min — escalate if SpO2 drops <92, SBP <100, or new AMS
Confirm patient not stuck behind now-tier queue beyond 1h abx window

why_this_tier: Hemodynamically stable: BP 118/72, SpO2 96%, RR 18, alert
No vital red flag triggered (no hypotension, hypoxia, or tachycardia >110)
Sepsis screen positive but without organ dysfunction signs — fits ESI 3 with workup
Needs ≥2 resources (labs + imaging + IV meds) → R-ESI-T3
would_upgrade_if: Lactate ≥2 or new altered mental status
Develops respiratory distress / accessory muscle use (asthma overlay)
would_downgrade_if: CXR negative AND lactate normal AND fever responds to acetaminophen — could trend toward viral URI/bronchitis, but still ESI 3 given abnormal exam
